---
title: "Appendix 1: Scenario 1 - Market Portfolio Perspective"
output: html_document
date: "2025-04-21"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load required libraries
set.seed(423)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(glmnet)
library(randomForest)
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastshap)
# ----------------------------
# Download and Load Dataset
# ----------------------------
# The Fama–French Global 3-Factor CSV is automatically downloaded via download.file()
# and unzipped using read_csv() with a 6-line skip to remove metadata.
# Columns are renamed to include standard identifiers (Mkt-RF, SMB, HML),
# and the date column is parsed into proper Date format using the lubridate package.
# A response variable (target) is then defined as the lead (next-month) of the market excess return,
# aligning with the out-of-sample prediction goal.
temp <- tempfile()
base <- "http://mba.tuck.dartmouth.edu/pages/faculty/ken.french/ftp/"
factor <- "Global_3_Factors"
format <- "_CSV.zip"
full_url <- paste(base, factor, format, sep = "")
download.file(full_url, temp, quiet = TRUE)
Global_3_Factors <- read_csv(unz(temp, "Global_3_Factors.csv"), skip = 6) %>%
rename(date = `...1`) %>%
mutate_at(vars(-date),~ as.numeric(.)) %>%
mutate(date = ymd(parse_date_time(date, "%Y%m", quiet = TRUE)))
# ----------------------------
# Prepare the Data
# ----------------------------
# Create a target variable: next month's Market excess return (Mkt-RF)
data <- Global_3_Factors %>%
arrange(date) %>%
mutate(target = lead(`Mkt-RF`)) %>% # Shift Mkt-RF by one period
filter(!is.na(target), !is.na(date)) # Remove rows with NA in target or date
# ----------------------------
# Function for Train-Test Split
# ----------------------------
# A custom split_data() function is created to divide the data into training and test sets.
# Two splits are tested: 70/30 and 80/20, using a fixed random seed for reproducibility.
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
set.seed(123)
split_data <- function(df, ratio = 0.7) {
n <- nrow(df)
train_indices <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(ratio * n))
list(train = df[train_indices, ],
test = df[-train_indices, ])
}
# Three models are trained for performance comparison:
# (1) OLS (baseline model) via lm()
# (2) LASSO regression using glmnet::cv.glmnet(alpha = 1) with 10-fold CV to select lambda
# (3) Random Forest using randomForest() with ntree = 500, mtry = sqrt(p), maxnodes = 30
# All models are evaluated on the hold-out test set using:
# - Mean Squared Error (MSE)
# - Mean Absolute Error (MAE)
# - R-squared (R2)
# These metrics are printed and summarized in a table for comparison.
# ----------------------------
# Research Question 1:
# Compare Baseline Linear Model vs. LASSO (70/30 Split)
# ----------------------------
split_70 <- split_data(data, ratio = 0.7)
train_70 <- split_70$train
test_70 <- split_70$test
# Define predictors and response for training/testing
x_train <- as.matrix(train_70 %>% select(SMB, HML))
y_train <- train_70$target
x_test <- as.matrix(test_70 %>% select(SMB, HML))
y_test <- test_70$target
# Baseline Linear Model (OLS)
lm_model <- lm(target~ SMB + HML, data = train_70)
lm_pred <- predict(lm_model, newdata = test_70)
lm_mse <- mean((lm_pred- y_test)^2)
lm_mae <- mean(abs(lm_pred- y_test))
lm_r2 <- 1- sum((lm_pred- y_test)^2) / sum((y_test- mean(y_test))^2)
lm_corr <- cor(lm_pred, y_test)
cat("Baseline LM MSE (70/30 split):", lm_mse, "\n")
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# LASSO Regression (using glmnet)
cv_lasso <- cv.glmnet(x_train, y_train, alpha = 1)
lasso_pred <- predict(cv_lasso, s = cv_lasso$lambda.min, newx = x_test)
lasso_mse <- mean((lasso_pred- y_test)^2)
lasso_mae <- mean(abs(lasso_pred- y_test))
lasso_r2 <- 1- sum((lasso_pred- y_test)^2) / sum((y_test- mean(y_test))^2)
lasso_corr <- cor(lasso_pred, y_test)
cat("LASSO MSE (70/30 split):", lasso_mse, "\n")
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ----------------------------
# Research Question 2:
# Compare LASSO vs. Random Forest (70/30 Split)
# ----------------------------
# Random Forest Model
rf_model <- randomForest(target~ SMB + HML, data = train_70)
rf_pred <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_70)
rf_mse <- mean((rf_pred- y_test)^2)
rf_mae <- mean(abs(rf_pred- y_test))
rf_r2 <- 1- sum((rf_pred- y_test)^2) / sum((y_test- mean(y_test))^2)
rf_corr <- cor(rf_pred, y_test)
cat("Random Forest MSE (70/30 split):", rf_mse, "\n")
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ----------------------------
# Research Question 3:
# Impact of Train-Test Split Ratios Using LASSO (80/20 Split)
# ----------------------------
split_80 <- split_data(data, ratio = 0.8)
train_80 <- split_80$train
test_80 <- split_80$test
x_train80 <- as.matrix(train_80 %>% select(SMB, HML))
y_train80 <- train_80$target
x_test80 <- as.matrix(test_80 %>% select(SMB, HML))
y_test80 <- test_80$target
cv_lasso_80 <- cv.glmnet(x_train80, y_train80, alpha = 1)
lasso_pred80 <- predict(cv_lasso_80, s = cv_lasso_80$lambda.min, newx = x_test80)
lasso_mse80 <- mean((lasso_pred80- y_test80)^2)
lasso80_mae <- mean(abs(lasso_pred80- y_test80))
lasso80_r2 <- 1- sum((lasso_pred80- y_test80)^2) / sum((y_test80- mean(y_test80))^2)
lasso80_corr <- cor(lasso_pred80, y_test80)
cat("LASSO MSE (80/20 split):", lasso_mse80, "\n")
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ----------------------------
# Summarize Results in a Table
# ----------------------------
results <- data.frame(
Model = c("Baseline LM", "LASSO (70/30)", "Random Forest (70/30)", "LASSO (80/20)"),
Train_Test_Ratio = c("70/30", "70/30", "70/30", "80/20"),
MSE = c(lm_mse, lasso_mse, rf_mse, lasso_mse80),
MAE = c(lm_mae, lasso_mae, rf_mae, lasso80_mae),
R2 = c(lm_r2, lasso_r2, rf_r2, lasso80_r2),
Corr = c(lm_corr, lasso_corr, rf_corr, lasso80_corr)
)
print(results)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ----------------------------
# Visualize Results: Bar Plots for MSE, R-squared, and MAE
# ----------------------------
# Results are visualized using ggplot2 as bar plots for MSE, MAE, and R2.
# This allows easy comparison across model types and train-test splits.
p1 <- ggplot(results, aes(x = Model, y = MSE, fill = Train_Test_Ratio)) +
geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
labs(title = "Model MSE Comparison",
x = "Model", y = "Mean Squared Error (MSE)") +
theme_minimal()
p2 <- ggplot(results, aes(x = Model, y = R2, fill = Train_Test_Ratio)) +
geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
labs(title = "Model R-squared Comparison",
x = "Model", y = "R-squared") +
theme_minimal()
p3 <- ggplot(results, aes(x = Model, y = MAE, fill = Train_Test_Ratio)) +
geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
labs(title = "Model MAE Comparison",
x = "Model", y = "Mean Absolute Error (MAE)") +
theme_minimal()
print(p1)
print(p2)
print(p3)
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ----------------------------
# SHAP Value Analysis for Model Interpretability
# ----------------------------
# To interpret model behavior, SHAP values are computed using the fastshap package:
# - For Random Forest: a prediction wrapper is used on the test subset
# - For LASSO: prediction extracted from glmnet.fit using best lambda
# SHAP scatter plots are shown to visualize feature influence.
# Define prediction wrappers for fastshap
# For Random Forest:
pred_rf <- function(model, newdata) {
predict(model, newdata = newdata)
}
# For LASSO: Use the cv.glmnet object; extract predictions from the underlying glmnet.fit.
pred_lasso <- function(model, newdata) {
as.vector(predict(model$glmnet.fit, newx = as.matrix(newdata), s = model$lambda.min))
}
# Compute SHAP values for a subset (first 50 rows) of test data for Random Forest
rf_sample <- test_70 %>% select(SMB, HML) %>% slice(1:50)
shap_rf <- explain(rf_model,
X = rf_sample,
pred_wrapper = pred_rf,
nsim = 50) # Number of Monte Carlo simulations
# Plot SHAP summary for Random Forest
plot(shap_rf, main = "SHAP Values - Random Forest")
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Compute SHAP values for LASSO on the same sample of test data
lasso_sample <- test_70 %>% select(SMB, HML) %>% slice(1:50)
shap_lasso <- explain(cv_lasso,
X = as.matrix(lasso_sample),
pred_wrapper = pred_lasso,
nsim = 50)
# Plot SHAP summary for LASSO
plot(shap_lasso, main = "SHAP Values - LASSO")
```

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# A scatter plot is created for LASSO predictions vs actual target values (70/30 split).
# Includes a diagonal red dashed line to visualize perfect prediction.
# Scatter Plot for LASSO (70/30 Split): Predicted vs. Actual
lasso_actual_vs_pred <- data.frame(Actual = y_test, Predicted = as.vector(lasso_pred))
ggplot(lasso_actual_vs_pred, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
geom_point(color = "blue", alpha = 0.6) +
geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
labs(title = "LASSO (70/30): Predicted vs. Actual",
x = "Actual Market Excess Return",
y = "Predicted Market Excess Return") +
theme_minimal()
```
